news_communities.title: 3 charged with human trafficking at Salem spas
news_communities.summary:
Police in Salem, Massachusetts, said three people are facing charges after a monthslong human trafficking investigation into several businesses.

Authorities said they investigated suspected trafficking and prostitution-related activities at businesses advertising “bodywork,” “reflexology” and “spa” services over the last three months.

Detectives executed search warrants Friday at businesses including New Custom Body Work and Red Rose Reflexology, both on Boston Street, police said Wednesday. They did not say whether more businesses were searched.

Salem police said 57-year-old Peter Leavitt of Peabody, 52-year-old Fang Liu of Peabody and 64-year-old Barry St. Onge of Shrewsbury were taken into custody and will be charged with trafficking of a person for sexual servitude, deriving support from prostitution and keeping a house of prostitution.

The Salem Police Department noted that human trafficking “involves the recruitment, harboring, transportation, provision, or obtaining of individuals for the purposes of forced labor or commercial exploitation.” It said in a press release that it “emphasizes that individuals involved in these situations are regarded as victims and are treated accordingly throughout the investigative process.”

Authorities did not say when the suspects would be arraigned or if they had attorneys.

“We see this as much a rescue operation as it is a criminal investigation,” Salem Police Chief Lucas Miller said in a statement. “The exploitation of victims of human trafficking will not be tolerated in the city of Salem.”

news_communities.title: East Boston getting popular gourmet doughnut/sandwich shop
news_communities.author: Boston Restaurant Talk
news_communities.summary:
[This story first appeared on Boston Restaurant Talk.]

The team behind a handful of gourmet doughnut shops and sandwich shops in the Boston area will be bringing the two concepts to a waterfront location.

According to a Facebook reel from Gallows Group founder Rebecca Roth Gullo, Blackbird Doughnuts and Sally’s Sandwiches will be opening in East Boston, moving into a space at Clippership Wharf. In the reel, Rebecca mentions that there will also be a “little sprinkling of The Gallows,” which was a gastropub that resided in Boston’s South End until closing in 2021, and that the business plans to open sometime in the late spring or early summer.

Locations of Blackbird Doughnuts can be found in Boston’s South End, Beacon Hill, Fenway, Brighton, Cambridge’s Harvard Square, Newton Centre, and Watertown, while Sally’s Sandwiches can be found in the South End, Harvard Square, and Brighton. The website for all locations can be found at blackbirddoughnuts.com.

news_communities.title: Chelsea High School teacher on leave after allegations of inappropriate behavior
news_communities.author: Griffin Gonzalez
news_communities.summary:
A high school teacher in Chelsea, Massachusetts, has been placed on leave after allegedly having inappropriate conversations with students.

Parents and students say the inappropriate behavior went on for years. The district took action after students staged a walkout at Chelsea High School this week.

“For the past three years, I’ve been enduring a teacher who has been inappropriate towards myself and other students,” 17-year-old Jelannie Hunter told the Chelsea School Committee this week.

“When you’re working with children, there are boundaries,” her mother told NBC10 Boston in an interview Thursday.

She said she went to the school for the past three years complaining of the teacher’s conversations with her daughter.

Students at Chelsea High School walked out of class Wednesday to protest the lack of action taken by the district despite complaints from students and parents.

“As of today, the employee involved has been placed on leave,” Chelsea Public Schools said in a letter to the Chelsea High School community Wednesday. “The school district has also initiated an independent investigation of the concerns raised regarding this matter.”

The district also said in the letter that while it “encourages and supports students’ constitutional rights to freedom of speech and expression,” it planned to discipline some students who stayed in the halls longer and “and disrupted the instructional day for themselves and others who did not participate.”

NBC10 Boston is not naming the teacher, as no charges have been filed against her.

We reached out to the district to see whether or not she would be paid throughout her leave and why action was taken now after years of complaint, but have not received a response.

news_communities.title: As Artemis II crew splashes down, Navy sailor from Mass. was set to greet them
news_communities.author: Thea DiGiammerino
news_communities.summary:
A U.S. Navy sailor from Massachusetts was expected to be among the first people to greet the Artemis II astronauts when they returned to Earth Friday evening.

Chief Hospital Corpsman Vlad Link is a member of the dive medical team that was set to open the Orion capsule, the U.S. Navy said in a media release. Link, who is from Chelsea, was expected to help get the astronauts out of the capsule safely after splashdown and make an initial medical assessment of their condition.

Link has 18 years of experience in dive medicine.

“I have been exposed to the Navy since I was a young teenager, and I’m proud to represent both my family and hometown,” Link said in the media release. “Contributing our efforts to NASA and the Artemis II mission is something we take great pride in as part of that legacy.”

NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Christina Koch and Victor Glover and Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen returned to Earth on Friday evening after 10 days in space. This involved a fiery plunge through the atmosphere, ending in a splashdown in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego.

news_communities.title: Teen held without bail on murder charge in Chelsea stabbing
news_communities.author: Mike Pescaro, Marc Fortier, Griffin Gonzalez and Sebastian Agudelo Gomez
news_communities.summary:
A teenager has been charged with murder in connection with a deadly stabbing on Thursday night in Chelsea, Massachusetts.

Sergio Josué Castellanos, 18, was held without bail at his arraignment Friday. He was allowed to hide his face in court.

Police responded to the intersection of Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue around 6:45 p.m. Thursday, and said multiple people were injured in stabbings.

Authorities said Friday that two people were injured: Castellanos, who suffered minor injuries, and 25-year-old Eduardo Rózales López of Chelsea.

Prosecutors with the Suffolk County District Attorney’s Office said López was treated at the scene for stab wounds to his neck and torso. He was transported to Massachusetts General Hospital, where he died from his injuries early Friday morning.

Reviewing video, police described seeing five people chasing after López. A man identified as Castellanos could be seen striking López in a stabbing motion while he’s on the ground, prosecutors said.

The video allegedly showed Castellanos and three other people fleeing. Police found him and three other males behind the fence of a home nearby on Chestnut Street.

Castellanos, who was tending to a stab wound to his leg, had changed his clothes from those seen on camera, which were found next to him, prosecutors said. He was transported to an area hospital for treatment.

Authorities said a black machete with stains on the blade was recovered.

Castellanos’ defense attorney, asking that his client be released on bail with GPS monitoring, raised questions about the identity of the attacker. He noted that in the video in which prosecutors described Castellanos making a stabbing motion, no knife was seen.

The judge ordered Castellanos held without bail.

Family members told Telemundo Nueva Inglaterra that López arrived from Guatemala seven years ago and leaves behind a 2-year-old child.

People in the area were shocked by the confrontation.

“It was kind of scary, I’m not going to lie. You still see the remnants of blood on the floor,” neighbor Shauniece White said Thursday. “It’s extremely concerning. As you see, it’s only been one nice day so far. If it’s already happening now, I can only imagine what the summer has in store for us. I’m hopeful that the police will come out and do due diligence to keep the community safe, but wishful thinking in a time like this.”

Massachusetts State Police are assisting Chelsea police with the investigation.

news_communities.title: Multiple people hospitalized after being stabbed in Chelsea
news_communities.author: Griffin Gonzalez and Mike Pescaro
news_communities.summary:
Police in Chelsea, Massachusetts, said multiple people were injured in stabbings Thursday evening.

Officers responded to the intersection of Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue around 6:45 p.m.

While the Chelsea Police Department did not say how many people were stabbed, it confirmed all those hurt were hospitalized, including one person who is in serious condition with potentially life-threatening injuries.

Authorities did not say whether anyone had been arrested.

“It was kind of scary, I’m not going to lie. You still see the remnants of blood on the floor,” said neighbor Shauniece White. “It’s extremely concerning. As you see, it’s only been one nice day so far. If it’s already happening now, I can only imagine what the summer has in store for us. I’m hopeful that the police will come out and do due diligence to keep the community safe, but wishful thinking in a time like this.”

Massachusetts State Police are investigating alongside the city’s department.

“Chelsea Detectives and Mass State Police Detectives are currently working this very serious incident and we are ready to utilize any and all of our resources to give our community the peace of mind our citizens deserve,” Chelsea Police Chief Keith Houghton said in a statement.

Anyone with information is asked to call 617-466-4800. Anonymous tips can also be left by calling 617-466-4880.

news_communities.title: ‘Horrific tragedy': New details in case of high school student charged with murdering Danvers woman
news_communities.author: Kaitlin McKinley Becker, Munashe Kwangwari and Marc Fortier
news_communities.summary:
An 18-year-old from Lynn, Massachusetts, was ordered held without bail on Friday after he allegedly murdered a woman insider her Danvers home in what authorities believe was a random attack.

The Essex County District Attorney’s Office announced Thursday night that Anthony DeMayo, a senior at Bishop Fenwick High School in Peabody, was being charged with murder in connection to the death of 68-year-old Janet Swallow.

DeMayo was arraigned Friday afternoon in Salem District Court. He pleaded not guilty and was ordered held without bail. He’ll be sent to Bridgewater State Hospital for a mental evaluation and is scheduled to return to court next month.

The case was called briefly just before 10:30 a.m., but the judge ordered DeMayo to be evaluated first to determine his competency.

When DeMayo returned to court for his arraignment later in the day, court psychologist Joyce Perotta told the judge she had interviewed DeMayo for about an hour with his lawyer present. She said he presented with depressive symptoms, including restricted affect, anhedonia and suicidal ideation, and it appears those symptoms have existed for some time.

Perotta said DeMayo demonstrated “adequate understanding” of the court proceedings but she had concerns about his rational understanding and the potential impact of his current mental state on his legal decision making.

She recommended sending him to Bridgewater State Hospital under tight security for further assessment.

The judge agreed with Perotta’s assessment and ordered DeMayo to be held without bail and sent to Bridgewater State pending a pretrial hearing on April 1. His attorney did not object to the order.

The investigation that led to DeMayo’s arrest began after a 911 caller told police that a man was acting erratically, walking around on Standish Road in Lynn with what looked like a blood-stained knife. Officers responded and found DeMayo, taking him to Salem Hospital.

DeMayor reportedly told Lynn police that he had killed a woman the night before in Danvers.

A search of DeMayo’s Lynn home revealed blood-stained clothing, and along with data from his cell phone, investigators contacted the Danvers Police Department, who conducted a well-being check and found Swallow dead inside her home at 17 Amherst St.

Prosecutors said Swallow was found dead, alone, “suffering wounds consistent with a homicide.” They said DeMayo apparently entered the home by climbing through a window and stabbed Swallow while she was in bed. They said no items were found to be missing from the home and they believe the attack was random.

The longtime Danvers resident was an ICU nurse at Lahey Hospital in Burlington and leaves behind two sons.

Scott James, Lahey’s chief nursing officer, released a statement Friday calling Swallow “a beloved and valued member of the Lahey Hospital & Medical Center community” who will be “greatly missed.”

“We share our deepest condolences with her family and loved ones and her colleagues in the Nursing Department and across our hospital. Our thoughts are with them during this very difficult time,” he added.

Officials said there is no known connection between DeMayo and Swallow. No other details about what happened have been shared by authorities.

Bishop Fenwick High School President Tom Nunan Jr. issued a statement Friday morning about the murder:

“We are aware of the investigation, and the information released regarding that investigation, by the Danvers Police Department and the Essex County District Attorney’s Office.

We know this matter involves serious charges against an 18-year-old student who is in the custody and care of the proper authorities.

The reports reveal the incident took place off campus, did not involve any other members of our school, did not seem to indicate any connection between the victim and the person being charged with the crime, and does not present any threat to the Bishop Fenwick community.

We are cooperating fully with local law enforcement in this investigation, which is ongoing. Because this is an active criminal investigation, we defer to the appropriate legal authorities to provide any additional information. We will not have further comment on the matter.

Our thoughts and prayers are with the victim, her family, and all who have been affected by this horrific tragedy.

As a community of faith and hope, our focus is on our faculty and staff, our families, and especially our students. We are here to help them, to provide for them the supports they need, and to be present for them in this terribly difficult moment.  To that end, additional counselors and other trained professionals will be on campus today and Monday.

Thank you for your understanding and your prayer.“

Danvers Police Chief Jamie Lovell also issued a statement expressing his department’s “deepest condolences” to the Swallow family, her friends and everyone who knew her.

“The loss is being felt across our town, and many are struggling to make sense of it,” he said.

news_communities.title: Danvers murder suspect was spotted wandering around Lynn before his arrest
news_communities.author: Amelia Fabiano
news_communities.summary:
A young man raised alarm on Thursday as he wandered streets and backyards in Lynn, Massachusetts, carrying a knife. Neighbors who saw him were shocked to learn that he’s accused in a murder in Danvers.

Multiple people told NBC10 Boston that they saw 18-year-old Anthony DeMayo walking around at about noon Thursday with a large knife in his hand. Later, he would be identified as the suspect in the murder of Janet Swallow in Danvers.

Several of them called 911, which led police to arrest him in a backyard on Standish Road. The whole thing was caught on home surveillance video.

“They said they saw a crazy guy with a knife, and I’m like, ‘How did this happen,’ you know? It’s scary, you never think, where you live, it’s going to happen,” John Bossi said.

Security cameras captured the moments leading up to the arrest. It starts with a vehicle crashing into Nells Pond in Lynn around 11:40 a.m.

“It’s hard to even get there. I don’t know how he got through. There’s no easy way for a car to get there,” Bossi said.

About 13 minutes later, DeMayo is seen walking down Bow Ridge Road in a bright green shirt, swinging a knife around in his right hand.

Then, he goes to Nells Pond Road. Ashley O’Brien was outside with her ducks when she saw him walk by.

“I thought for sure that this guy was obviously mentally unwell. I didn’t want to make a scene and run away or anything like that, cause he made eye contact with me,” she said.

She immediately called 911, the first of several calls from the neighborhood.

“I just waited till he passed my house, went in, told my husband and called the cops,” O’Brien said. “When we came back out, he was just kind of stabbing all the bushes outside and running a knife across the houses and stuff like that — it was very bizarre.”

Rhonda Deparolesa was another on nearby Standish Road.

“I was sitting on my chair, in the recliner, and all of the sudden, I see somebody jump over my fence,” Deparolesa said. “He just laid flat on his back on the bulkhead and just wasn’t moving, so my husband was upstairs, and I said, ‘Something’s going on here.'”

Deparolesa said he tried to open her door at one point, but thankfully, it was locked.

“What if he did get in my house? My husband and I might not be here,” she said. “You know, you think you’re safe in your house. Sometimes you’re not.”

By around 12:30 p.m., police were after him. They found him in a backyard a few doors down from Deparolesa’s home. He was arrested and placed in a police cruiser.

“I’m just absolutely shocked that a young kid, 18 years old, had his whole life ahead of him, and had to go and do this,” Deparolesa said. “For what? Why?”

Everyone who spoke to NBC10 Boston was shocked to learn DeMayo is accused of murder.

“I just thought this guy needed some help and I just called. I did not think that he’d just murdered somebody,” O’Brien said.

Police confirmed DeMayo was a student at Bishop Fenwick High School in Peabody. They did not comment on the investigation, but said they are fully cooperating with local law enforcement.

Their thoughts and prayers are on the victim, her family, and everyone impacted by the horrific tragedy. They said their focus right now is on staff and students.

They had additional counselors on campus Friday and will on Monday, as well.

news_communities.title: New details revealed in court about Malden teacher accused of raping high school student
news_communities.author: Kaitlin McKinley Becker
news_communities.summary:
A 54-year-old teacher accused of raping a high school student in Malden, Massachusetts, faced a judge Thursday, a day after his arrest.

Authorities allege Scott Marino gave alcohol to an 18-year-old who is known to him and then sexually assaulted her last Wednesday, Feb. 18, at his Stoneham home, where the victim was currently living. Marino was arrested a week after the alleged incident and has been charged with two counts of rape, one count of indecent assault and battery and one count of furnishing alcohol to a person under the age of 21, according to the Middlesex County District Attorney’s Office.

Prosecutors provided shocking new details Thursday in Woburn District Court at the arraignment for Marino, who teaches at Linden STEAM Academy in Malden and coaches the girls’ basketball team at Malden High School.

Resources for victims of sexual assault are available through the National Sexual Violence Resources Center and the National Sexual Assault Telephone Hotline at 800-656-4673, and Massachusetts provides this list of statewide and resources for sexual assault survivors.

Jacob McCrindle of the Middlesex County District Attorney’s Office said in court that the alleged victim in this case had been in the foster care of Marino and his wife at the time this occurred. The victim, who moved in with the Marinos at their Stoneham home in the fall, was a basketball player for Marino during the season, and the two were known to each other for many years.

According to prosecutors, Marino’s wife was scheduled to take a trip out of state last Wednesday. The victim was on school vacation, so she went with Marino and a grandfather who lives in a basement apartment at the home to an Ash Wednesday mass. They dropped the older man off at home, and then Marino and the victim went driving because she had a learner’s permit and was learning to drive on the highway. They made the decision to go into Boston to get a takeout seafood dinner, and on the way home, Marino had them stop at a liquor store where surveillance video shows he purchased a strawberry margarita mix, a large bottle of Patron tequila and some other agave liquor.

The two then went back to Marino’s Stoneham home and he allegedly gave her a large margarita. She drank some of it but didn’t like it, though Marino allegedly encouraged her to keep drinking. He later poured them shots of tequila, and they ate the seafood dinner they got. The victim later told police that she was feeling ill so she went to her bedroom where she vomited many times, eventually lying down in bed.

Marino then came in and sexually assaulted her two times, prosecutors said in court. The victim later retrieved her cell phone and began texting friends and ultimately an aunt, begging for help.

McCrindle said that the aunt called 911 as she was driving to the Marino’s home, and the victim was taken to Winchester Hospital for a full sexual assault examination.

“This is a relationship where the defendant was the victim’s foster parent, he took her into his home, that’s a position of trust, as well as coupled with the fact that the defendant is a teacher, or was a teacher at the Malden Public Schools and he was also her coach for many years,” McCrindle said in court, arguing for $100,000 cash bail. “The defendant used I would suggest that relationship, he took advantage of that relationship, there’s a significant age difference, the defendant is 54 years old, the victim is 18 years old. There’s also a significant size difference that’s described in the police report between the defendant and the alleged victim…. it’s also noteworthy that the defendant appears to have waited until his wife left the home so he was more isolated with the victim…clearly some planning went into this.”

Marino’s defense attorney argued before the judge that he’s a respected educator with deep community support who has “never been in court in his life.”

The judge ultimately ordered $75,000 cash bail based on the gravity of circumstances along with several conditions requested by the commonwealth, including that Marino stay away and have no contact from the victim and any witnesses, stay away from all Malden school property and events, be fitted with a GPS bracelet to enforce exclusive zones including the victim’s home address, her school, her workplace and a gym that she frequents, surrender his passport and not be allowed to leave the state.

The judge also added the condition that Marino be alcohol free and submit to SCRAM testing.

Resources for victims of sexual assault are available through the National Sexual Violence Resources Center and the National Sexual Assault Telephone Hotline at 800-656-4673, and Massachusetts provides this list of statewide and resources for sexual assault survivors.

Malden Superintendent Timothy Sippel previously said the district was informed Wednesday that Marino had been taken into custody in connection with “a serious incident alleged to have occurred outside of working hours and involving one of our high school students.”

Marino was immediately placed on leave, the superintendent added, noting that the district is cooperating fully with law enforcement agencies in their investigation.

“I understand how upsetting and distressing this is to our community given the serious nature of these allegations,” Sippel said in a statement. “The safety of our students and staff is our highest priority.”

A plea of not guilty was entered on Marino’s behalf. He is due back in court for a probable cause hearing on April 16.

news_communities.title: Malden teacher and coach charged with rape of teen
news_communities.author: Mike Pescaro
news_communities.summary:
A teacher and coach in Malden, Massachusetts, has charged with the rape of an 18-year-old.

Malden Public Schools said Scott Marino had been arrested over a “serious incident” involving a high school student. The Middlesex County District Attorney’s Office and Stoneham Police Department later said he had been charged with two counts of rape, one count of indecent assault and battery and one count of furnishing alcohol to a person under 21.

Marino is accused of providing alcohol to the victim, who is known to him and sexually assaulting her on Feb. 18, prosecutors said.

Superintendent Timothy Sippel said Marino teaches at Linden STEAM Academy — which serves kindergarten through eighth grade — and is the head coach of the Malden High School girls’ basketball team.

Sippel said that Marino’s “arrest is in connection with a serious incident alleged to have occurred outside of working hours and involving one of our high school students.”

“I understand how upsetting and distressing this is to our community given the serious nature of these allegations,” the superintendent said.

Marino is due to be arraigned Thursday in Woburn District Court. It was not immediately clear if he had an attorney.

news_communities.title: Girl rescued after falling through the ice in Malden
news_communities.author: Thea DiGiammerino and Mike Pescaro
news_communities.summary:
Firefighters rescued a teen girl who had fallen through the ice into the frigid water of a pond in Malden, Massachusetts, on Wednesday afternoon.

It happened around 2 p.m. at Fellsmere Pond, fire officials said. Firefighters in water rescue suits went in to find the girl holding onto the edge of the ice, according to the Malden Fire Department. She was pulled from the water in under 30 seconds.

“I don’t even think the engine stopped,” Malden Fire Captain Roy Tribble said. “I jumped up, I sprinted down here, pretty much just jumped right into the water.”

The 14-year-old girl was conscious and alert when she was brought to a Boston hospital for treatment.

Tribble said the water was about 30 feet deep.

“A life was saved. It was a young girl who fell through the ice,” added Captain Bob Hegarty.

He warned everyone to stay off thin ice.

“A warning to everybody out there that ice, if it’s not thick enough, you’re going to fall through, and if you fall through, you’re not going to be able to get yourself out,” Hegarty said. “So stay off the ice until it’s very thick, which — who can tell when it’s very thick?”

news_communities.title: Malden woman accused of threatening federal agents making immigration arrest
news_communities.author: Thea DiGiammerino
news_communities.summary:
A Massachusetts woman is accused of threatening to kill federal agents who were conducting immigration enforcement in Malden.

Bethany Abigail Terrill, 37, was charged with threatening a United States official, the U.S. Attorney’s Office said Thursday.

Prosecutors say that Terrill approached the agents as they were making an arrest outside Malden District Court on Sept. 29, describing her as verbally abusive. She allegedly shouted things like “ICE is here, ICE is here,” “You guys are monsters, this is insane,” “Sir, what’s your name, what’s your name,” “I can try to help you,” and “I am an American civilian, I have a right to be here,” and called the agents Nazis.

She is further accused of trying to push past the agents, yelling “Charlie Kirk died, and we love it… We’re coming for you, gonna kill you.” 

According to the statement of probable cause, an agent heard the threat and proceeded to detain Terrill, who they said was “actively resistant and fought to escape.”

Officials say the agents were wearing body cameras, and Terrill was also filming at the time. In the statement of probable cause, investigators say the threat can be heard in the footage from Terrill’s phone.

The agents on scene were from multiple agencies, including Homeland Security Investigations (HSI), ICE and the FBI.

During her appearance in U.S. District Court in Boston on Thursday, a public defender said Terrill owns a salon and was struggling with legal issues in an unrelated case.

Judge Paul Levenson ordered Terrill be released with conditions, saying “federal agents have a right to not be subjected to threats.”

Terrill was ordered as part of her conditions of release to participate in a mental health program for treatment on a regular basis, to develop a safety plan for her own safety and others, avoid contact with potential witnesses, have no contact with any federal official, family or otherwise in any way or form, refrain from alcohol and the use of any illegal drugs, to avoid contact with law enforcement, and to refrain from making any further threats.

She didn’t speak to reporters outside of court.

The charge of threatening a U.S. official carries a sentence of up to 10 years in prison, three years of supervised release and a fine of $250,000.

news_communities.title: Cities bordering Everett unite in Revolution soccer stadium transparency push
news_communities.author: Asher Klein
news_communities.summary:
Four cities that border Everett, Massachusetts, have banded together in a push for more involvement in the New England Revolution’s plans for a soccer-specific stadium in the city.

The mayors of Revere, Medford, Malden and Chelsea formed a coalition called City Mayors for Citizens that they said will seek transparency on plans for the stadium, including the impacts it will have on their communities.

The 25,000-seat stadium proposal has progressed in the state Legislature but still faces plenty of hurdles. The City Mayors for Citizens said they are worried about how the building on along the Mystic River might impact local infrastructure, public transit and quality of life.

“A project of this size and scale will have significant regional impacts,” said Revere Mayor Patrick Keefe Jr said in a statement. “We want to ensure that a stadium is contributing positively to the communities it is drawing resources from. Our residents should not bear the burdens of a private stadium that has strong potential to negatively affect their day-to-day lives.”

Asked for a response to the statement, a Revolution club spokesperson noted that it expects to work with neighboring communities after it reaches agreements with Everett and Boston, which would allow the property to have its port designation removed and the process of construction to continue.

The spokesperson also noted that the team thanked the mayors and promised to review the concerns raised in their letter, sent Monday.

news_communities.title: Video shows Salvadoran man being detained by ICE in Malden
news_communities.author: Oscar Margain, Marc Fortier and Mark Garfinkel
news_communities.summary:
The wife of a Salvadoran man says he was violently taken into custody by immigration officials on his way to work in Malden, Massachusetts, on Wednesday morning.

The incident occurred shortly before 6 a.m. in the area of 28 Kennedy Drive.

Leslie Perlera Gonzalez said she received a distressing call from her husband, Edgar Hernan Elias Escobar, shortly before 6 a.m. as he was heading to his construction job. She rushed to Kennedy Drive, where she said Elias Escobar was surrounded by immigration officers.

“I heard them smash his window, and he was saying, ‘Ow, you’re hurting me,'” Perlera Gonzalez said. “When I arrived on the scene, he had a Taser on his face.”

The 31-year-old had no criminal record and was in the process of adjusting his immigration status after the couple married last year, noted Perlera Gonzalez.

“He’s never come close to doing anything that would be considered a crime,” she said.

She alleged that agents used an unmarked car to ram her husband’s vehicle, forcing him off the road before detaining him.

Malden police said Elias Escobar sustained minor injuries. He was transported on a stretcher to a hospital in Melrose, where Perlera Gonzalez said she was not allowed to see him.

About an hour later, he was moved to a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement processing facility in Burlington.

A spokesperson from the Department of Homeland Security said in a statement that Elias Escobar was the target of the ICE operation and suffered “no injuries.”

“On September 17, 2025, ICE conducted an immigration enforcement operation in Malden, Massachusetts to arrest Edgar Hernan Elias-Escobar, an illegal alien from El Salvador. During the arrest, the illegal alien refused to comply with officer commands to rolldown his window. The officers took appropriate action and followed their training to use the minimum amount of force necessary to resolve the situation in a manner that ensured the success of the operation and prioritized the safety of the public and our officers,” the spokesperson said in a statement. “Out of an abundance of caution, ICE law enforcement escorted Elias-Escobar to the hospital for evaluations. Medical staff found he had no injuries and discharged him. He was then escorted to the Boston Filed office for processing and will be placed in removal proceedings.”

“I’ve been expecting this from somebody I know every day since Donald Trump was elected,” said Amiee Parco, an attorney with Greater Boston Legal Services and a colleague of Perlera Gonzalez. “I’ve been waiting for it to affect me personally.”

Friends and coworkers gathered outside the Burlington facility, joining a weekly protest to support Elias Escobar’s release and that of other detainees.

“He’s a really wonderful person,” said Alyssa Rao, a friend of the couple. “I think this is going to impact everyone.”

After Wednesday’s ordeal, Perlera Gonzalez fears what her husband is going through while detained.

“I’m terrified of what could be happening when no one is watching,” she said.

At last check, Escobar was still at the facility. It’s unclear how long he’ll be held — often, people stay several days, though the facility is meant to detain people for hours.

His lawyer is trying to prevent the government from sending him to a far-away detention center, and from deportation.

ICE has ramped up arrests across greater Boston in recent weeks under “Operation Patriot 2.0,” detaining immigrants in public spaces.

Gov. Maura Healey spoke out against the sweeps on Tuesday, saying federal immigration enforcement officers are “creating an incredible amount of fear and intimidation.”

“Some of it seems purposeful, to be honest. Why else are you parked outside a church? Why are you parked outside a community center?”

The governor said her office has received no information from ICE about who is being detained or why.

“This is not a furtherance of public safety,” she said. “In fact, it undermines public safety.”

Asked what she can do to protest White House policy, Healey said: “I join with other governors in urging the Trump administration, in the strongest possible terms, to change course.”

State House News Service contributed to this report.

news_boston.title: FDA says Taylor Farms cyclospora finding was a false positive, investigation continues
news_boston.author: Alexis Garcia
news_boston.summary:
A lettuce sample supplied by Taylor Farms de Mexico that initially tested positive for cyclospora, the parasite linked to a growing “explosive diarrhea” outbreak, was later determined to be a false positive, the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) said in a statement on Sunday.

The FDA said laboratory experts reviewed again the test results and concluded the finding “does not represent true amplification and should be considered a false positive.” The agency removed information about the sample from a previous outbreak update and said there are currently no confirmed positive product samples for cyclospora, the parasite that causes cyclosporiasis.

Taylor Fresh Foods, the parent company, said the FDA notified them Saturday that one of its iceberg lettuce products from central Mexico had tested positive before later correcting the result.

The development comes days after Taylor Fresh Foods announced a voluntary recall of iceberg lettuce sourced from central Mexico following an FDA traceback investigation that identified Taylor Farms de Mexico as a common supplier linked to illnesses.

Recalls Jul 17

Taylor Farms recalls iceberg lettuce amid cyclosporiasis outbreak

Health & Wellness Jul 17

Lettuce at Taco Bell in 5 states confirmed as a source of diarrhea-causing parasite

Health Jul 14

Nearly 7,000 cases of cyclosporiasis confirmed or under investigation nationwide, CDC says

The company said it removed all iceberg lettuce from the region from the U.S. market after federal officials pointed to a specific farm as a potential source of the outbreak.

On Friday, the FDA advised consumers not to eat shredded iceberg lettuce from Mexico served at Taco Bell locations in Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio and West Virginia. The agency said its investigation found convergence on Taylor Farms de Mexico as the supplier of shredded iceberg lettuce served at Taco Bell restaurants where some people became ill.

The FDA said it is continuing to work with Taylor Farms to ensure any products implicated in the outbreak are removed from the market while federal and state investigators continue collecting and testing samples.

Nearly 7,000 people may have been sickened nationwide in the broader cyclosporiasis outbreak, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. As of Friday, the CDC had confirmed 1,644 cases and was investigating more than 5,100 additional illnesses. No deaths have been reported.

news_boston.author: Kaitlin McKinley Becker
news_boston.summary:
A person was critically injured when they were struck by a vehicle on Interstate 93 in Quincy, Massachusetts, shortly after their ride share driver had called state police with concerns about their erratic behavior Sunday night.

State police say they received a 911 call shortly after 9 p.m. from the driver who had safety concerns about their passenger, and a trooper subsequently located the vehicle near Exit 8 on I-93 south.

When the trooper attempted to speak with the passenger, police say they fled on foot. Moments later, they were struck by a passing vehicle.

The victim was taken to a Boston hospital with life-threatening injuries, according to police.

The right and middle lanes were closed near the scene after the crash. Crime scene services, collision analysis reconstruction, and detectives from the Norfolk County District Attorney’s Office all responded.

Further details were not immediately available, and police cautioned the information they’ve provided at this point is preliminary as the incident remains very active.

An investigation is ongoing.

news_boston.title: Boston celebrates Spain-Argentina World Cup final: ‘Amazing vibe'
news_boston.author: Erin Logan
news_boston.summary:
It was a match up of soccer royalty on Sunday when Lionel Messi and defending champion Argentina played Spain in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final — both teams looking for glory on the world’s biggest stage.

Celebrations were underway around the world, including right here in Boston, as Spain was crowned the tournament champion after outlasting Argentina 1-0 in extra time.

Fans packed watch parties to witness history, and Boston was fired up. There was a massive final party outside Boston City Hall, where fans told NBC10 Boston they’ve had the best time this World Cup — especially when the Tartan Army was here.

Another thing people were happy about is that so many have become soccer fans since experiencing all of this excitement for the past several weeks.

“This is an amazing vibe. Everything’s amazing right now,” JT Tessema said. “As like someone who has watched soccer all my life, I’m actually so happy that everybody from America is actually seeing how big soccer is. Like growing up not that many people actually took soccer as more serious as basketball or like American football but now you see all the different cultures, all the different communities in one space, it’s just like amazing.”

Tessema said Boston wasn’t quite the same after Scotland left: “I’m not gonna lie. The streets have been a little bit more quiet, but we still got a lot of people as we can see right now.”

“We are from Spain. We work for one week in each city. This is our last city and this is Boston and also the final World Cup,” another man said. “It’s crazy because we’ve been in New York and it was like more Argentina than Spain and this is the change. It’s more Spanish.”

“I’m more of a Portugal fan and unfortunately my team did not qualify to the finals so I’m supporting the stadium,” Mrudula Chilukuri said. “When the Scots were here, we were partying until 2 a.m. so we need more of that culture here now I don’t know like maybe bring the Scots here again whenever possible. It was really fun. Even the Scots loved Boston and I spoke to a lot of them. They traveled to multiple cities here in the US, but they loved Boston the best. I hope they’re back soon. It will be really fun. if not for FIFA, for like you know just to hang out so it was really fun to be a host city.”

It will be interesting to see what or who will replace this level of excitement, as there’s still several more weeks of summer.

news_boston.title: Will Lindsay Clancy take stand during her trial? Jury selection starts Monday
news_boston.author: Mary Markos
news_boston.summary:
Jury selection starts Monday in the case against Lindsay Clancy, the Duxbury, Massachusetts, mother accused of strangling her three children.

Clancy has pleaded not guilty to those murders. Her trial is expected to last weeks, focusing on one central question: was she in control of her actions the night her three children died?

Before that question can be tackled, though, the court has to fill the jury box. A judge is looking for 18 people who can put aside their emotions and decide this case based solely on the evidence.

Legal experts say that process could take up to two weeks. The trial itself could last two months.

When testimony begins, the focus won’t be on whether Clancy killed her three children in their Duxbury home more than three years ago. It will be on whether she had the mental capacity to understand what she was doing and stop herself from doing it.

Her attorneys plan to argue she didn’t, using what’s called a lack of criminal responsibility defense.

One of the most important witnesses could be Patrick Clancy, the father of their children.

“I think that probably will be the most difficult testimony we see over the course of what likely is a couple of months here,” NBC10 Boston’s chief legal analyst Michael Coyne said.

Will Clancy take the stand in her trial?

“I don’t know, that’s a really difficult question and that’s really at the end of the day for her to decide in consultation with Kevin Reddington, but it’s not the lawyer’s call, it’s hers,” Coyne added.

Clancy is paralyzed from the waist down after jumping from a second-floor window the night her children died. Her attorney has said she was suffering from postpartum depression and was overmedicated when she allegedly strangled 5-year-old Cora, 3-year-old Dawson and 8-month-old Callan.

news_boston.title: Shenna Bellows, Nirav Shah and Dan Kleban all drop out of Maine's Senate race
news_boston.author: Bridget Bowman | NBC News
news_boston.summary:
Former state Senate President Troy Jackson is racking up support to become Maine’s next Democratic Senate nominee, with several of his opponents ending their campaigns on Sunday, including Maine Secretary of State Shenna Bellows, former Maine public health official Nirav Shah, and Maine Beer Company owner Dan Kleban.

“Donald Trump’s attacks on our democracy are only accelerating, and the best way that I can serve our state and country at this moment is by focusing exclusively on my role as Secretary of State,” Bellows said in a statement. “This has been an unprecedented nominating process, compressed into days instead of months, and I’m grateful to every volunteer who worked around the clock for this movement. That energy is exactly what we need to beat Susan Collins in November — and Democrats don’t have a day to waste in unifying around that shared goal.”

“A few weeks ago, I entered this race because I could not accept the idea of Susan Collins gliding to a sixth term at the very moment Maine families are being forced to pay the price for her votes. Today, I am stepping aside, and I am doing it for exactly the same reason that led me to get in: we must defeat Susan Collins, and the Democratic Party must be unified to do it,” Shah said in his statement. “As we turn to defeating Susan Collins, Mainers must remember that she is not a moderate. When the most extreme policies have threatened Maine, she has repeatedly failed to stop them. She voted for the Iraq War, gave ICE billions with no meaningful guardrails and helped put Brett Kavanaugh on the Supreme Court before Roe was overturned. Again and again, she has sided with Donald Trump when Maine needed her to stand up to him. After nearly thirty years, Susan Collins has given Mainers more excuses than independence. That’s why I am suspending my campaign for the United States Senate today and endorsing Troy Jackson. Susan Collins, Donald Trump, and Senate Republicans are banking on disunity and frustration from Maine Democrats to try and win this seat, and we are not going to give them that. Maine Democrats are united, angry, and energized.”

“It’s bittersweet that just as we’ve collected more than enough signatures to qualify for the ballot, it’s also become clear from the county nominating meetings that we lack a path forward. That’s why I’m ending my campaign for the United States Senate,” said Kleban in his statement. “I ran for the Senate because Susan Collins has stopped doing what’s right. For nearly 30 years, she has enabled a system that is rigged against hardworking Mainers. We deserve a senator who fights for us, not one who enables Trump at every turn and does the bidding of her billionaire backers…From the very beginning, my focus has been on defeating Susan Collins. I will do whatever I can between now and November to help Troy Jackson finally achieve that goal.”

Democratic delegates at next week’s state convention will select a new nominee to take on GOP Sen. Susan Collins, after primary winner Graham Platner abruptly ended his campaign last week after an allegation of sexual assault, which he denied.

While both Shah and Kleban endorsed Jackson in their statements on Sunday, Bellows did not mention him.

Jackson’s slate of delegates, however, captured nearly all of the slots up for grabs in Saturday’s county meetings. Those delegates are not required to support Jackson at the convention, even if they were elected as part of his slate.

Jackson expressed confidence on Sunday morning at a rally with his supporters outside the York county meeting.

“Yesterday was an unbelievable huge step, but our work is not finished — not even close,” Jackson said, noting more counties were voting on Sunday.

But he sounded like a likely nominee.

“Every candidate and every supporter deserves our respect, because this moment, I know for a fact, is way bigger than any one candidate, certainly, is way bigger than I have ever been,” Jackson said. “And a lot of people poured their hearts in this movement, before I even entered this race.”

Jackson later hinted at winnowing field, telling reporters at the county meeting, “If we have a day like [Saturday], the rest of the candidates will drop out and we’ll just be getting the nomination next week.”

Jackson said he had heard from a couple candidates who expressed their “congratulations.” He said they “pretty much” suggested they would end their campaigns before the convention, but declined to say who he spoke with.

Shah, Bellows and Jackson all competed in the governor’s race, but did not win the nomination. All three candidates support similarly progressive policies, like Medicare for All and abolishing the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency.

They were able to quickly spin up their old campaigns after Platner’s exit, and Jackson has stressed that his team focused on organizing his slate of delegates ahead of county meetings this weekend.

“As soon as we got the delegate list, I mean, I was calling, people were calling to find out where people stood and if they needed any more information about me,” Jackson told reporters Sunday, the former logger pointing to his union roots as a strength.

“In the union, that’s what we do. We organize and go after it because it’s so important,” Jackson said.

But Jackson could still face competition at the convention.

Jordan Wood, a former congressional staffer who is running for Senate, told NBC News at the York County meeting that he plans to stay in the race. He raised concerns about Jackson’s vocal support for Platner, suggesting Republicans could use those ties against Jackson if he is the nominee.

“I think that Troy can beat Susan Collins. I think it’s going to be difficult, though, because he helped introduce us to Graham and got a lot of — conveyed a lot of trust and authority onto him, as did Bernie Sanders,” Wood said. Sanders endorsed both Platner and Jackson in their primaries earlier this year, but has stayed neutral after Platner ended his Senate bid.

Jackson “can overcome it, but it’s going to be tough,” Wood said.

Some Mainers who supported Jackson’s opponents are beginning to view Jackson as their likely nominee.

“At the end of the day, this entire process is about whose campaign can organize the best effort,” said Shaun Donnelly, a 44-year-old computer graphics artist who had been running to be a Bellows delegate from York County. He had been supporting Bellows because after Platner, he “was not excited about getting behind another white dude.”

“If the numbers are true and Troy has the the majority of the activists in the party behind him, those are the people who we need energized to win in the fall,” Donnelly said.

“So I think overall it’s a good thing, and I look forward to voting for him if he wins,” Donnelly said.

NBC10 Boston contributed to this report

news_boston.title: Rising boxer Hannah Rapp killed in Texas after police say driver accelerated in reverse and struck her bicycle
news_boston.author: Mirna Alsharif and Austin Mullin | NBC News
news_boston.summary:
An up-and-coming boxer was killed Saturday after a driver struck her with his car while she was riding her bike in Brazos County, Texas.

Deputies with the Brazos County Sheriff’s Office responded to a report of a major crash involving a vehicle and a bicyclist on the 23000 block of FM (Farm to Market Road) 159 at around 10 a.m.

The bicyclist, 26-year-old Hannah Rapp, was found lying in the roadway and unresponsive after being struck by a vehicle, according to a probable cause statement. Rapp was taken to an area hospital, where she was pronounced dead.

The driver, 31-year-old Charles Medina, had passed two bicyclists — Rapp and her boyfriend — while traveling along FM 159 at a high rate of speed, per the probable cause statement.

Rapp’s boyfriend “threw his arms up and hollered towards the defendant,” Deputy Sheriff Mike Stumpf said in the statement. “The defendant slammed on his brakes and skidded to a stop before putting his vehicle into reverse.”

Medina then “rapidly accelerated in reverse and struck Rapp with his vehicle, knocking her backwards into the roadway,” Stumpf said.

Speaking to an investigator, Rapp’s boyfriend told an investigator that Medina had driven at a high rate of speed within about a foot of them, prompting him to throw his hands up and yell.

He said Rapp’s head struck the car’s rear window when Medina backed into her.

Medina told the sheriff’s office that he thought the cyclists were “trying to flag him down” so he “stepped on his brakes which caused them to lock up before coming to a stop,” according to the probable cause statement. He also claimed he did not see Rapp when he was backing up “until the last moment before he hit her,” and was traveling at a speed of 15 mph.

Medina’s 2008 Honda was observed at the scene with a hatch that was crushed inwards and a shattered rear window, according to the sheriff’s office. Skid marks showing that Medina slammed on his brakes and rapidly accelerated backwards were also spotted.

“The angle of the reverse skid marks appeared as if the defendant was steering towards something while backing up,” Stumpf said, adding that the evidence on the scene contradict Medina’s claim that he reversed at a low speed.

Medina was arrested and charged with manslaughter, according to online records. He is being held at the Brazos County Detention Center.

“The investigation remains active and ongoing as detectives continue to gather evidence and review all available information,” the sheriff’s office said.

Rapp was building a name for herself in the boxing world, challenging World Boxing Council women’s featherweight champion Tiara Brown for the title just last month. The June 13 bout, held in Florida and broadcast on ESPN, ended in a unanimous-decision loss for Rapp.

In a statement, the WBC remembered Rapp as “a relentless warrior inside the ring” and “a beacon of light whose dedication and charisma left an indelible mark” on both the North American Boxing Federation and the WBC itself.

“The loss of Hannah Rapp leaves us heartbroken,” WBC President Mauricio Sulaimán said in a statement. “She was an exceptional boxer, but above all, an invaluable member of our boxing family. We join in the grief that overwhelms her loved ones, her team, and all those who had the privilege of knowing and supporting her throughout her brilliant career.”

Supporters flooded Rapp’s Instagram with condolences following news of her death.

“Rest in peace,” one commenter wrote under her most recent post. “You had so much ahead of you and were such an inspiration.”

news_boston.title: Inside the 2026 World Cup final halftime show: Shakira, Madonna, BTS, Bieber and more
news_boston.author: Alexis Garcia
news_boston.summary:
The FIFA World Cup 2026 final got the Super Bowl treatment for the first time ever, turning soccer’s biggest stage into a global celebration of music, culture and unity.

The 11-minute spectacle featured performances from Madonna, Justin Bieber, BTS, Shakira and Burna Boy, while also squeezing in soccer royalty, children’s choirs, the Muppets and a little “Ted Lasso” charm.

The show at MetLife Stadium, home to the NFL’s New York Jets and New York Giants, was an attempt to bring Super Bowl-style energy to the World Cup, but the performance was considerably more packed than the usual 13-minute American football counterpart. Each headliner spent about two minutes performing.

Madonna opened the show with her 2000 hit “Music” alongside Brazilian soccer legends Ronaldo and Ronaldinho. It began with a prerecorded roller-rink sequence before the singer rode into the stadium on a dune buggy, singing, “Music, makes the people come together.”

Venezuelan conductor Gustavo Dudamel and the Electric Mayhem band from “Sesame Street” followed with au a rendition of the White Stripes’ stadium anthem “Seven Nation Army.”

A nod to Norway’s viral Viking row introduced BTS, who took over the pitch with a performance of “Dynamite.” The K-pop supergroup was joined by a large group of dancers as fans throughout the stadium sang along.

Justin Bieber followed after an introduction from “Ted Lasso” stars Jason Sudeikis and Brendan Hunt, who appeared in character and held up a playful “BELIEB” sign. Accompanied by an acoustic guitar, Bieber performed “Everything Hallelujah,” changing the tempo with an acoustic performance.

2026 World Cup Jul 16

Ranking the top goals from the 2026 World Cup: Messi, Mbappe, Cape Verde on list

World Cup 4 hours ago

Future host locations for the men's FIFA World Cup after 2026

2026 World Cup 6 hours ago

Spain tops Lionel Messi, Argentina 1-0 to win 2026 World Cup

Shakira and Burna Boy then brought the energy back up with “Dai Dai,” the official song of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Joined by various dancers, including a group of children, the pair performed the tournament anthem that was heard before all 104 matches during the competition.

The show closed with Coldplay frontman Chris Martin, Dudamel, the PS22 Chorus of elementary school students from Staten Island and a cast of Muppets and “Sesame Street” characters, including Kermit the Frog, Miss Piggy and Cookie Monster.

The colorful finale underscored a theme woven throughout the performance: unity and love.

The pregame spectacle

The star-studded entertainment began well before kickoff with a variation of anthems.

Jennifer Hudson delivered a live rendition of “The Star-Spangled Banner,” an unusual addition for a World Cup final, where the host nation’s anthem is not traditionally performed.

Spain’s “La Marcha Real” was performed by Spanish Brass, while Argentine singer María Becerra sang Argentina’s “Himno Nacional Argentino.”

Tennis star Carlos Alcaraz and “Squid Game” actor Jung Hoyeon then presented the World Cup trophy in its Louis Vuitton trunk before Tom Cruise took the field to introduce the finalists.

“Today only two teams remain: España!” Cruise told the crowd before adding, “y Argentina!” He later called on fans to celebrate “a tournament that brought the world together.”

The musical lineup also included internet personality IShowSpeed, who performed his World Cup-themed song “Champions,” and Post Malone, who debuted new track “Chrome Heartbreaker” before reuniting with Swae Lee for their hit collaboration “Sunflower.”

Robbie Williams also took the stage to perform FIFA anthem “Desire,” joined by Nicole Scherzinger and Italian singer Laura Pausini.

news_boston.title: Missing Warren man with dementia found dead: ‘Not the outcome we prayed for'
news_boston.author: Kaitlin McKinley Becker
news_boston.summary:
A missing elderly man with dementia has been found dead more than 10 days after he wandered from his home in Warren, Massachusetts.

The Warren Fire Department announced that Elliot Blackwell had been located around 3:22 p.m. Sunday by a K9 search and rescue team, and tragically pronounced dead. Teams had been searching for the 91-year-old since he left his Cutler Road home on Wednesday, July 8. Police said at the time they believed he may had gone walking in the woods behind his house.

Fire officials did not say where Blackwell was found Sunday.

“Undoubtedly, this was not the outcome that we had hoped and prayed for, but this discovery has been able to provide closure to the family,” the fire department said in a press release posted to Facebook, noting that search and rescue teams had navigated terrain over the past 11 days conducting strategic searches in hopes of finding Blackwell alive. “We are extremely grateful for all of the local, state, and volunteer organizations that have worked extensively to bring this missing persons case to a resolution.”

The fire department also thanked the public for their support throughout this trying time, and went on to ask everyone to keep comments respectful, or avoid commenting at all, out of respect for the family.

Warren police called the update very sad, saying their thoughts and prayers are with the Blackwell family.

news_boston.title: Future host locations for the men's FIFA World Cup after 2026
news_boston.author: Sanjesh Singh
news_boston.summary:
And that’s a wrap on the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

After 104 games, nearly 300 goals and Spain topping Argentina to become champions out of 48 teams, the tournament came to a close.

The next World Cup will actually be the 2027 women’s competition in Brazil next summer. But the next men’s tournament is now another four years away.

So, where does the future of the tournament lie after being held across the U.S., Mexico and Canada? Here’s everything to know so far:

2030 World Cup: Spain, Portugal, Morocco, Argentina, Uruguay, Paraguay

Believe it or not, the number of “hosts” for the next tournament is double that of 2026. There will be six nations staging games, and three different continents are involved.

Spain, Portugal and Morocco are confirmed to host the majority of the games, so southern Europe and northern Africa will be in the spotlight.

And because this tournament will be the 100-year anniversary of the first ever World Cup, Uruguay, Argentina and Paraguay will each host one “celebratory game.”

Uruguay and Argentina initially launched the first bid in 2017, but eventually FIFA President Gianni Infantino favored the trio of Spain, Portugal and Morocco in 2019. But through this six-country method, every nation is satisfied in some capacity.

2034 World Cup: Saudi Arabia

Due to FIFA’s confederation rotation policy for the tournament, the 2034 edition could only be hosted by a team in Asia or Oceania. Uzbekistan and Kazakhstan discussed a joint bid that ultimately was abandoned, as well as a 10-plus-team Southeast Asian bid that included Cambodia, Thailand, Vietnam and more.

New Zealand also pushed for a bid on the Oceania side, with Australia and Indonesia involved in possible joint efforts. Ultimately, Saudi Arabia continued its mission to grow in the sports world by running unopposed to earn the rights as New Zealand dropped off. It will be Saudi Arabia’s first time as hosts.

The tournament will likely be played in the winter due to the extreme heat in the region, similar to Qatar 2022. However, Ramadan is in November and December of 2033 and 2034, so early 2035 could also be a possibility as things are fluid.

2038 World Cup: TBD

No host is currently confirmed for the 2038 tournament, which is still a decade away. If the current rotation model is applied, Concacaf or Oceania regions may be first in line due to three continents being involved in 2030, though it’s not exactly clear how that’d work yet.

That means another U.S.-Mexico-Canada World Cup could reoccur if approved, though no country — or countries, in this case — has ever hosted two World Cups in the span of just 12 years.

A report in 2025 said that New Zealand may return to the picture with a joint bid, with Fiji and the U.S. in the mix. Germany has also announced interest in either 2038 or 2042, though it is awaiting more concrete details from FIFA.

news_boston.title: Usha Vance gives birth to fourth child
news_boston.author: Rebecca Shabad | NBC News
news_boston.summary:
Second lady Usha Vance gave birth Sunday to her fourth child, Vance’s office announced on X.

“We are excited to announce that our baby boy, Alec Neel Vance, was born this morning,” the Vance family said in a statement. “Usha and the baby are happy and healthy, and our kids are overjoyed to meet their little brother.”

Vance, 40, is the first woman since the 1870s to give birth while her husband is vice president.

She and her husband, Vice President JD Vance, have three other young children, two boys and one girl, who are ages 4 to 9.

While promoting his new book this year, Vance previously said it was unlikely that he would take parental leave once the baby arrived. The couple announced the pregnancy in January and said at the time that she was due in late July.

In March, the vice president, who could run for president in 2028, said at a White House event in Michigan that he had to persuade his wife to have another child.

“I remember when we decided to run for vice president, I said, ‘Honey, I really want to have a fourth kid.’ And she said, ‘Well, you can become vice president or you can have a fourth baby,’” Vance said. “But, ladies and gentlemen, I am persuasive, because I got both.”

In an interview with NBC News later that month, Usha Vance confirmed that he did convince her to have a fourth child, though she said that she had “never closed the door on that.”

She said that after having their daughter, she thought their family was complete. “But as time passed,” she said, “I realized that I was feeling more and more, kind of, excited about that possibility” of having another one.

“And so if there was a chance, I should take it, and I knew that I’d be happy if we only had three kids, and I knew that I’d be happy if we had four. And so here we are.”

This comes amid the news that the vice president and second lady are considering leasing a house in Middleburg, Virginia, separate from the vice president’s residence at the Naval Observatory in Washington, D.C. The Secret Service is planning to provide additional security for the rural property.

news_boston.title: Spain tops Lionel Messi, Argentina 1-0 to win 2026 World Cup
news_boston.author: Sanjesh Singh
news_boston.summary:
The World Cup title is going to Spain.

Spain took down reigning champion Argentina and Lionel Messi 1-0 in the 2026 final in New York/New Jersey on Sunday.

Ferran Torres scored the winning goal all the way in the 106th minute, as Spain capitalized on Argentina being down to 10 men following Enzo Fernandez’s late second yellow card.

Messi and Co. could not become the first team to repeat as World Cup champions in 64 years, as their tally stayed at three wins all time.

Spain now has two in two, its last win coming in the 2010 final — also a 1-0 extra-time win, that time vs. the Netherlands.

Spain head coach Luis de la Fuente started the game with the same team that eliminated top European nations in Belgium and France, the latter being the 2018 runner-ups.

Lionel Scaloni made three changes for Argentina, bringing Rodrigo De Paul into the right-midfield slot, Gonzalo Montiel at right-back and Nico Gonzalez on the left flank.

The game opened hot in the first 10 minutes as Lamine Yamal helped create some opportunities despite failing the final action.

And that would essentially be it from the first half, as neither side really took the initiative with the margins swinging in Spain’s favor.

The second half stayed with Spain, but still no goals could be found despite heavy waves of pressure.

Fernandez’s second yellow card came late in regulation, but it definitely paved how extra time would go as Argentina sat back to try to head to penalties. His first card was for dissent, as he argued with the referee after a foul. He then swept Pau Cubarsi’s legs in a dangerous landing for the second card.

MORE WORLD CUP COVERAGE

World Cup 21 hours ago

The flight hours and miles of World Cup travels of FIFA's Infantino in numbers

2026 World Cup Jul 18

How the World Cup final is starting to look a lot like the Super Bowl

2026 World Cup Jul 17

World Cup final history: List of winners and more to know before Argentina-Spain

Nico Williams had a disallowed goal in the first half of extra time while Merino just sent a header wide of the right post.

Torres then found the breakthrough early in the second half of extra time, eventually sealing the deal. Messi and Argentina got on the ball a bit more to try to salvage the game, but it just wasn’t going to be.

Spain ended the tournament with the toughest route, having played Uruguay, Austria, Portugal, Belgium and France en route to the final. Unai Simon and Co. conceded just one goal, which was in the 2-1 win to Belgium as Merino scored the late winner. The nation also never trailed at any point, a remarkable statistic.

Possession can sometimes be the best form of defense. It’s the philosophy that Spain practices the best.

Spain ended the game having 65% of the ball with 1.94 expected goals across 20 shots and 31 touches in Argentina’s box, per data-tracking platform FotMob.

Argentina never got into the game until it needed an equalizer. La Albiceleste ended with just 0.2 expected goals on two shots, with Messi seeing one shot blocked by Mikel Merino’s face.

La Furia Roja opened the tournament with the infamous 0-0 draw to Cabo Verde, which sparked Vozinha’s rise to stardom at 40 years old.

Argentina topped its group that featured Algeria, Austria and Jordan, with knockout wins featuring Cabo Verde, Egypt, Switzerland and England. Every game ended up being close, with two comebacks and two extra-time thrillers involved.

Messi will end his World Cup run at 39 and one triumph. He lost in 2014 and 2026 to similar scorelines and teams, the former being a 1-0 loss to Germany.

He delivered eight goals and four assists, being the heartbeat of his nation despite his age. He came in second of the Golden Boot race as Kylian Mbappe’s two goals in the third-place game Saturday put the French star on top.

Spain also swept the major awards from the tournament: Pau Cubarsi won the Young Player award, Unai Simon claimed the Golden Glove and Rodri secured the Golden Ball.

news_boston.title: Red Sox beat the Rays 6-1 behind Contreras and Gray for their 13th straight win
news_boston.author: The Associated Press
news_boston.summary:
Willson Contreras hit a two-run homer, Sonny Gray gave up one run and five hits over six innings, and the Boston Red Sox beat the Tampa Bay Rays 6-1 on Sunday for their 13th win in a row.

The streak is tied for the second-best in franchise history with a 13-game run in July 1948. The franchise’s longest was 15 games in June 1946.

Gray (12-1) struck out five and issued four walks in his ninth consecutive quality start. He has not allowed more than three runs since April 14.

After Victor Mesa Jr. hit an inside-the-park homer in the second inning to give the Rays a 1-0 lead, Andruw Monasterio tied it in the bottom half with an RBI double.

Boston scored three more in the third without a hit. Jahmai Jones came home on a passed ball and Wilyer Abreu scored on a fielder’s choice. Junior Caminero’s throw struck Abreu in the back as he slid into home, allowing all runners to advance safely.

Contreras scored from third on Romy Gonzalez’s ground out to make it 4-1.

Contreras swung on the first pitch he saw in the fifth for a two-run, 414-foot homer to the triangle in Fenway’s center field.

Rays starter Shane McClanahan (8-6) allowed six runs (four earned) on five hits in five innings. He struck out three and walked three.

Up next

The Rays head to Toronto for a four-game set with the Blue Jays, with RHP Nick Martinez (8-2, 2.65 ERA) taking the hill against RHP Dylan Cease (6-4, 2.56).

The Red Sox continue their 10-game homestand with a three-game series against the Orioles. LHP Payton Tolle (5-6, 3.11 ERA) starts opposite RHP Shane Baz (4-9, 4.19).

news_boston.title: Panasonic toaster ovens sold on Amazon, Costco recalled nationwide
news_boston.summary: If you bought a Panasonic electric toaster oven online for about $170, check the model number — 11,480 are being recalled for a shock or fire risk.

news_boston.title: U.S. military recovers unidentified remains in Jordan as another service member dies in Iraq
news_boston.author: Mosheh Gains, Courtney Kube and Gordon Lubold | NBC News
news_boston.summary:
The U.S. military announced Sunday it recovered unidentified human remains while searching for an American service member reported missing after an Iranian missile and drone attack in Jordan, as another U.S. service member was killed in a separate incident in Iraq.

U.S. Central Command said military personnel found the remains Sunday at the site of Friday’s attack in Jordan. Officials said an examination is underway to determine whether they belong to the missing service member.

The announcement came a day after CENTCOM said two U.S. service members were killed and a third was missing following Iran’s July 17 attack on U.S. and partner forces in Jordan.

Separately, CENTCOM said a U.S. service member was killed Saturday in northern Iraq during the controlled detonation of unexploded ordnance from a downed Iranian one-way attack drone. A second service member suffered a minor injury and remains under medical care.

The mounting death toll has effectively ended the interim ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran negotiated last month. After weeks of trading accusations that each side has violated the agreement, the two countries resumed attacks, with U.S. forces striking Iranian military targets and Tehran launching missiles and drones at U.S. allies in the region.

Late Saturday, CENTCOM said U.S. forces completed the eighth consecutive night of launching munitions against Iran, targeting Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces believed to have been involved in Friday’s attack on U.S. service members in Jordan.

The military said that the two service members killed died while defending against an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack. Four other U.S. service members were medically evacuated to hospitals in Jordan, but have since been discharged.

This is a developing story. Please check back for updates.

War with Iran 20 hours ago

U.S. strikes Iran's Revolutionary Guard over attack that killed troops in Jordan

War with Iran Jul 18

U.S. military launches new airstrikes to ‘punish' Iran for deaths of U.S. troops

news_boston.summary: AIPAC and its affiliated groups have spent close to $30 million backing Rep. Haley Stevens in the Michigan Senate Democratic primary, their largest investment ever in a single race.

news_boston.summary: The Boston Red Sox have turned their season around, climbing from the bottom of their division to playoff contention with a 12-game winning streak.
